title: 10 possible side effects of Linum usitatissimum (Flaxseed) if used improperly
intro: Linum usitatissimum, also known as flaxseed, can have several potential negative health effects if used improperly. First, consuming large amounts of flaxseed can cause digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and diarrhea. Additionally, flaxseed can interfere with the absorption of certain medications, so it's important to consult a healthcare provider before adding flaxseed to one's diet if taking medication. Moreover, flaxseed contains phytoestrogens, which can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, and may be harmful in large quantities, particularly for women with a history of hormone-sensitive cancers. Additionally, flaxseed should not be consumed raw or unripe, as it contains potentially harmful compounds that can cause respiratory and gastrointestinal symptoms. Finally, it's crucial to drink plenty of water when consuming flaxseed, as it can expand in the digestive tract and cause blockages if not properly hydrated.
